title: Kota Gullu
tagline: Kota Gullu — spiritual town of Telangana

overview.short: Ghanpur temples, popularly known as Kota Gullu, are a group of 12th-century stone temples that are located in Ghanpur near Warangal in Telangana, India.
overview.description: Ghanpur temples, popularly known as Kota Gullu, are a group of 12th-century stone temples that are located in Ghanpur near Warangal in Telangana, India. They are located 9 km northwest of the more famous Ramappa Temple in Palampet.

topPlaces.name: Kakatiya Thermal Power Station
topPlaces.description: Kakatiya Thermal Power Station is located near Chelpur village of Ghanpur mandal in Jayashankar Bhupalpally district of the Indian state of Telangana.

topPlaces.name: Pandavula Gutta
topPlaces.description: Pandavula Gutta (also Pandavula Guttalu or Pandavula Guhalu) is an ancient geological site and cave located near Kothapally village in Regonda mandal of Jayashankar Bhupalpally district in the Indian state of Telangana. It was designated as the only geoheritage site of Telangana.

topPlaces.name: Ramappa Temple
topPlaces.description: Ramappa Temple, also known as the Rudreshwara Temple, is a Kakatiya style Hindu temple dedicated to the Hindu god Shiva, located in Palampet village, Mulugu district, Telangana, India. It is 15 km (9.3 mi) from Mulugu, 66 km (41 mi) from Warangal, 209 km (130 mi) from Hyderabad.

itinerary.title: Day 1 — Kakatiya Thermal Power Station & around

items.note: Kakatiya Thermal Power Station is located near Chelpur village of Ghanpur mandal in Jayashankar Bhupalpally district of the Indian state of Telangana.

Pandavula Gutta (also Pandavula Guttalu or Pandavula Guhalu) is an ancient geological site and cave located near Kothapally village in Regonda mandal of Jayashankar Bhupalpally district in the Indian state of Telangana. It was designated as the only geoheritage site of Telangana.

Ramappa Temple, also known as the Rudreshwara Temple, is a Kakatiya style Hindu temple dedicated to the Hindu god Shiva, located in Palampet village, Mulugu district, Telangana, India. It is 15 km (9.3 mi) from Mulugu, 66 km (41 mi) from Warangal, 209 km (130 mi) from Hyderabad.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Kota Gullu?
faq.a: The best time to visit Kota Gullu is Oct – Mar. Summers are 26–40°C and winters are 12–28°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Kota Gullu?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Rajiv Gandhi International Airport, Hyderabad (194 km) and the nearest railway station is Secunderabad Junction (Hyderabad) (176 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Kota Gullu?
faq.a: 2 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Kota Gullu at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Kota Gullu?
faq.a: Stays in Kota Gullu start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Kota Gullu?
faq.a: The top attractions are Kakatiya Thermal Power Station, Pandavula Gutta, Ramappa Temple.
